name: Patrol
description:
Library component. Models the patrol scheduler that invokes `gt sling` for the same formula+agent. Real implementation: witness/refinery patrol-cycle loops invoke `gt sling <patrol-formula> <agent>` to keep an active hooked-mol on the patrol agent (mol-deacon-patrol, mol-witness-patrol, mol-refinery-patrol).

v0.5 (defense-doc liveness fix, take 3): Patrol no longer observes Agent state. The Pending->Slung fire is monotonically enabled, so WF_vars(Patrol_Step) guarantees firing.

Why this design path: v0.3 observed Agent=Resumed (oscillating enable, WF doesn't force firing). v0.4 added a latch (Pending -> Armed -> Slung), but the latch transition itself had the same oscillating-enable problem (Patrol could miss every Resumed visit). Without SF support in Kinner today, the only way to make WF force firing is to make the enable monotonic from initial state. v0.5 achieves this by dropping agent observation entirely.

Fidelity tradeoff: real witness/refinery patrols ARE driven by a scheduler that nominally watches agent state, but the timing isn't load-bearing for the #3768 bug -- the bug is about the close-step inside sling.go, not about when Patrol decides to fire. The model captures the bug regardless of Patrol's timing relative to the substrate. The substrate (Hook+Agent+HandoffCycle+Mailbox+Pane) still cycles for narrative completeness but doesn't gate Patrol firing.

actors.name: Patrol
actors.owner: Patrol scheduler. Fires Sling once when WF picks it; terminal Slung.
